title: Governed Execution and Learning Loop
summary: Parent initiative for a governed AI execution loop that selects work, runs bounded implementation, evaluates outcomes, extracts reusable learning, and promotes changes only after evidence and human approval.
original_problem: RTS has valuable loop, audit, memory, skill, provider, and success-replication assets distributed across repositories, but no single governed end-to-end operational loop joins them safely.
why_it_matters: A complete loop can shorten repeated development while preventing autonomous scope expansion, lost context, unverified learning, and PR #91-style hidden-coupling failures.
reason_frozen: The feasibility survey found four independently verifiable construction boundaries. Building the parent directly would combine asset discovery, read-only evaluation, execution control, and skill promotion into one unsafe integration.
preserved_value: The complete two-loop architecture: execute safely, evaluate evidence, propose improvements, compare versions, require human approval, and preserve rollback/reconstruction.
trigger_conditions: All four children have current evidence-backed assessments
Read-only loop data demonstrates value before execution authority expands
negative_triggers: Any attempt to flatten all repository bodies into RTS core
Missing rollback or human approval boundary
Private repository content would be exposed
